entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Your Feedback Matters - Multi-language captions for SAP Learning
entry.content:
We have exciting news for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customers - and we need your input to make it even better!

The SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG team are thrilled to announce that we now have the capability to provide multi-language captions for our SAP Learning videos. This is a big step forward in making our many how-to videos on SAP Learning more accessible, inclusive, and impactful for learners around the globe.

These how-to videos are provided supplementary to SAP Learning Journeys and SAP Learning Courses. They bring you deeper how-to expertise with demonstrations on targeted topics.  The aim is to speed up your innovation adoption.

You can see an example of this on Getting Started with SAP Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition – which is now available with captions in English, German, Spanish, and Portuguese (Brazil).

You choose your caption language Captions in the Settings of SAP Learning video player as shown on the video Getting Started with Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - German language is selected and German captions can be seen overlaying the video

But here's the thing — we want YOU to help us prioritize.

Why Multi-Language Captions Matter

Learning complex enterprise software like SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is challenging enough without the added barrier of a language gap. Whether you're an end user, a consultant, or an IT professional, being able to follow along in your preferred language can make all the difference between confusion and confidence.

Multi-language captions mean:

  • Greater accessibility for non-native English speakers
  • Better knowledge retention when learning in your native language
  • Wider reach and easier reuse for SAP Learning content
  • A more inclusive learning experience for everyone

Which languages would you like to see?

This is just as important as the content itself! Whether it's German, French, Spanish, Simplified Chinese, Japanese, Portuguese, or another language entirely — your feedback will help us shape our roadmap.

Where Do You Want Us to Start?

With dozens of SAP Learning videos already created for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, we want to focus first on what matters most to you. Here are some areas we're considering — but we'd love your thoughts!

Joule Videos

Are the videos covering Joule, SAP's AI copilot, at the top of your list? As AI-driven features become increasingly central to the SAP experience, understanding Joule in your own language could significantly enhance adoption and productivity across your organization.

For example:

  • Getting Started with SAP Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Resolving Missing Business Roles for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

These supplement Joule courses such as New Course: Getting Started with Joule for Business Users and Course: Introducing Joule 

More videos coming soon!

Fiori Videos

Do Fiori videos take priority for you? With SAP Fiori being the primary user interface for SAP S/4HANA, having captions in multiple languages could help countless users navigate/configure/activate/extend/support and make the most of SAP Fiori launchpad, apps, and classic UIs. For example:

Introductions for Business Users, Key Users, and Consultants:

  • Introducing SAP Fiori Launchpad for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Creating SAP Fiori Launchpad Layouts for Custom Business Roles
  • Extending SAP Fiori Object Pages with Adapt UI
  • Extending SAP Fiori List Report Apps with Adapt UI
  • Extending SAP Fiori Overview Pages with Adapt UI

How-to guidance for Administrators, Architects, and Support teams:

  • Patching SAP Fiori (SAPUI5)
  • Activating SAP Fiori Launchpad Using SAP Fiori Foundation Task List
  • Configuring the SAP Fiori Launchpad for SAP S/4HANA
  • Bringing Apps Onto SAP Fiori Launchpad
  • Activating SAP Fiori Content in Custom Business Roles
  • Enabling the 'Share: Microsoft Teams' Functionality for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA
  • Integrating Contact and Presence Info from Microsoft for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA

These supplement Learning Journeys such as Learning Journey: SAP Fiori - System Administration and Learning courses such as Learning the Basics of SAP Fiori and Discovering SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics

Other Videos

Is there another category of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition videos that you feel is more urgently needed? Is there a video you need that you would like to see created? For example, topics in:

  • Finance and Controlling
  • Supply Chain and Procurement
  • Enterprise Asset Management
  • System Administration
  • Configuring Integration with other Cloud solutions
  • Using SAP Activate for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Projects

Some examples:

  • Configuring Situation Handling for Maintenance Task Lists in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management
  • Implementing Cross-App Navigation Control for Fiori Applications (“Open In” Capability) in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management
  • Enabling Advanced Shipping & Receiving Capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FPS0
  • Setting up the Data Replication Framework for Product Replication from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Sales/Service Cloud Version 2
  • Mastering the Planning and Execution of a Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (exclusive content)

These supplement Learning Journeys such as Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Sourcing and Procurement, Implementing Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and many more.

What's most valuable to your role and your organization?

This is your opportunity to directly influence what we bring as SAP Learning how-to videos to you and your teams.

Share your Feedback

Please take a moment to share your thoughts by answering these quick questions in the comments below:

  1. Which SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition video topics are your top priority for multi-language captions?
  2. Which specific languages are most important to you or your organization?
  3. Is there anything else you'd like us to consider as we start to add captions.
  4. Do you have proposals for new videos we can consider for our roadmap ?

Every comment counts. Whether you're a customer, an SAP partner, an independent learner or an employee — your voice helps us build a better learning experience for everyone.

Drop your thoughts in the comments below — we can't wait to hear from you!

Becoming a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Guru

Follow our tag S4HANA RIG - SAP Community for more from the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG

Find more of our blogs, videos, whitepapers and other accelerators in our SAP Community topic pages:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Getting Started with SAP Joule for Consultants Expert Workspace
entry.content:
This blog is meant to be a quick read — the idea is to introduce SAP Joule for Consultants expert mode over your morning coffee or tea 🍵, and kick off a new day of exploring how AI can support SAP consulting work in a more practical, domain-focused, and project-relevant way.

In complex SAP transformation programs, especially in areas such as system migration, Clean Core governance, Finance processes, or Supply Chain domains, general knowledge is often not enough. What project teams really need is the ability to combine SAP public knowledge, senior consultant experience, enterprise-specific governance rules, and real project constraints into one consistent decision-making process.

This is exactly where SAP Joule for Consultants Expert comes into the picture. I am very excited to share this new feature with you right away.

Domain-specialized intelligence

SAP Joule for Consultants Expert can provide dedicated experts per domain, such as a Clean Core Expert or Finance Expert. This is important because every SAP domain has its own terminology, tools, decision patterns, and governance requirements.

Knowledge fusion

SAP Joule for Consultants Expert combines SAP public knowledge with uploaded domain assets, such as consultant best practices, methodology documents, governance guidelines, project templates, and lessons learned. In this way, valuable consulting knowledge can be reused instead of staying only in individual experts’ experience.

Decision framework and guardrails

A strong expert should not only answer questions. It should guide decisions. SAP Joule for Consultants Expert can include priority rules, decision trees, safety rules, compliance boundaries, and governance logic. This makes the output more consistent and easier to explain to project teams and stakeholders.

Like the other features of SAP Joule for Consultants, the Expert Workspace is also very easy to get started with:

Step 1: Go to SAP Joule for Consultants Console and create a New Expert

Step 2: Give your expert a name and add a short description

Step 3: Maintain Custom Instruction

Custom Instructions usually consist of Persona, Goals, Guardrails, Output Format, Context Instructions, Process Tasks, Language, and Global Deliverables.

Persona & Goals: Define who this expert is, who they support, and what they do.

Example: You are a senior SAP consultant with 10+ years of experience in SAP S/4HANA Conversion and Transformation topics in the area of logistics. Your goal is to help users understand the various SAP S/4HANA Conversion approaches, provide guidance for each Simplification Item, consistency error, SAP Reference Business Architecture, and compatibility scope item of logistics modules. 

Guardrails: Define what the expert must never do and how to handle missing information. Always follow compliance rules, never go beyond the defined scope.

Example: Do not use the retired Clean Core Tier concept. Use the Clean Core Level concept only. Do not generate ABAP Code unless explicitly requested. If requested: code + explanation + Clean Core assessment.

Output Format: Response structure, file formats, templates.

Process Tasks: Step-by-step workflows, sequential activities | "first", "then", "finally", "steps".

Language: Locale requirements, communication style.

Step 4: Attachments/uploaded files

Uploaded files are another key part of the expert workspace.

They provide grounding information for the expert, such as domain-specific knowledge assets or project-specific documents. From a consulting perspective, this is essential. A recommendation based only on public information may be too generic. But a recommendation based on public SAP knowledge plus project-specific assets can become much more precise and useful.

Step 5:  Now, your first SAP Joule for Consultants Expert has been created. Go ahead and try it out!

New Conversation → Quick Links → Show Experts → Select your Expert → Shoot your first prompt

Note: Currently, only one Expert can be selected per conversation.

Happy testing!

To summarize

  1. SAP Joule for Consultants Expert is a domain-focused digital consultant for SAP projects.
  2. Custom instructions matter as it defines the expert’s role, workflow, and guardrails, so the response style and decision logic remain consistent across consulting tasks.
  3. Uploaded files matter because it provides the grounding for domain knowledge and assets.
  4. With this approach, consulting knowledge can become a reusable digital asset.

Of course, a single weapon is not enough — we also need to leverage all the SAP Joule for Consultants capabilities to deliver a true combination punch:

SAP Joule for Consultants

Onboarding Guide for SAP Joule for Consultants

SAP Joule for Consultants Console 

SAP Joule for Consultants Expert Workspace

SAP Joule for Consultants Application & benefits in Implementation projects

Now you’ve probably finished your coffee or tea 🍵. And I hope this quick overview has given you a clear taste of what an SAP Joule for Consultants Expert is trying to achieve.

At this point, I have to say that I’m truly impressed by how quickly SAP Joule for Consultants delivers new features. And I can’t wait to share SAP Joule for Consultants Expert use cases with you in the next blog! 🎇 

Let’s keep moving forward on the SAP transformation journey — learning, improving, and building smarter together! 🚀

entry.title: Choosing the Right TMS SAP S/4HANA: Why one Size Rarely Fits all
entry.content:
Recently, I was approached by the supply chain leadership team and the head of IT of one of the top European giants to shortlist five transportation planning solutions. The company was midway through an SAP S/4HANA migration. Their legacy TMS was to be replaced for expiring software and support issues. Every planning vendor was telling them that they can integrate seamlessly with S/4HANA. Although they all integrate, that does not mean it is the right fit.

The chemical company had freight mix like most shippers I worked with in the past, having full truckloads, less than truckloads, and small parcels. They moved FTLs between DCs and major customers. The used LTL carriers for regional replenishment to smaller retail locations. And they shipped thousands of small parcels daily direct to consumers, parts for warranty replacement, or samples through UPS, FedEx, and regional carriers. So fundamentally, three transportation planning problems. They needed one technology to address all of them within an S/4HANA environment.

After working for 18 years with multiple industries implementing SAP Transportation Management and Order to Cash processes across manufacturing, retail, and third-party logistics, I have learned that the biggest mistake companies make in TMS selection is treating it as a single problem. It is rarely one; FTL planning, LTL optimization, and small parcel management, each one by itself, are unique opportunities. Generally, one software decision for all freight modes is often not right.

Every transportation planning stack relies on a central decision-making layer that drives optimization, execution, and responsiveness. The system that holds the optimization logic, the carrier master, the lanes, the rates, and the planning rules. When you run SAP S/4HANA, you have a choice to choose if you want the decision-making inside the SAP ecosystem or if you want it in a best-of-breed external TMS that connects to S/4HANA as its backbone.

This is not a technical question. It is a strategic one. The answer shapes every other decision.

If the decision-making lives in SAP, specifically in SAP TM, which is embedded in S/4HANA, you get native data flows, a single master data model, tight integration between order management, inventory, realistic ATP, and transportation, and no middleware layer to maintain between your planning system and your ERP. The trade-off is generally SAP TM’s optimization capabilities, while robust, are not specialized like some best-of-breed tools that offer transportation-specific reporting, parcel optimization, and overall market freight rate intelligence (highs and lows).

If the decision-making lives in a best-of-breed TMS system a platform like Blue Yonder, MercuryGate, Oracle TMS, or Uber Logistics they typically get more sophisticated optimization engines for specific modes or network configurations as a vendor whose product specifically focuses on transportation. The trade-off is integration complexity, duplicate master data, and the ongoing cost and maintenance support for the connection between your TMS and S/4HANA.

The company was leaning towards best of breed. By the end of the evaluation, they chose SAP TM for their FTL and LTL operations and a dedicated parcel management solution for their small parcel volume integrated into S/4HANA through an API.

 

The three freight mixes are three different decisions.

Full truckload (FTL) planning is fundamentally about lane selection, load optimization, and carrier selection against the freight contracts. The key variables are weight, cube, origin-destination pairs, and service level commitments. SAP TM was designed around this problem and handles it in depth with multi-leg chains, intermodal transfers, freight tendering, and freight order management. For FTL networks of any complexity, SAP TM is a strong native choice within S/4HANA.

Less than truckload (LTL) is a different optimization challenge, as you are not moving a dedicated truck from original to destination; rather, you are sharing trailer space with other shippers, which means your cost and transit time depend on carrier networks. The freight classification by National Motor Freight classification code, dimensions, and accessorial charges that vary by carrier in ways that are difficult to model cleanly. LTL rating is complex because of deficit weight calculation, weight breaks, base rates, discount structure, FAK (Freight All Kinds Agreements) for specific product freight classes, and accessorials like liftgate, inside delivery, and other surcharges interact differently across every carrier.

SAP TM can manage LTL freight orders and carrier selection, but its native LTL rating engine has significant configuration effort to replicate the full complexity of real-world LTL tariff structures. Several clients I have worked with have handled this by integrating a specialized LTL rating API or Idocs with tools like SMC3’s RateWare or a third-party rating engine to feed accurate LTL rates into SAP TM's carrier selection logic, while keeping the freight order and execution management inside SAP TM. This hybrid approach generally preserves the S/4HANA integration benefit while closing the LTL rating gap.

Small Parcel operates under a totally different set of rules. Parcel carriers UPS, FedEx, USPS, DHL, and other regional operators operate on a published rate, dimensional weight, zone pricing, residential and fuel surcharges, and service tiers that vary by carrier. So, the main challenge is not lane planning like it is in freight. It's choosing the right carrier and service for each individual shipment, often at very high volume and in real time.

SAP TM is not really built to compete with specialized parcel platforms in that space. Tools like ShipERP, XPS ship, or EasyPost are made specifically for carrier connectivity, live rate shopping, label printing, and tracking. If a company ships a few hundred parcels or more each day, the better approach is usually a dedicated parcel platform connected to S/4HANA, so S/4HANA can still manage the order, cost, and tracking date without trying to do everything itself.

The real question is not which system should handle every mode. It is how to connect the right tool for each shipping mode back to S/4HANA as the main source of truth for cost, delivery, and customer communication.

For my client, that changed the whole evaluation. Instead of trying to force one TMS to cover everything, we designed a layered setup. SAP TM for FTL and managed LTL, a parcel platform for small parcels, and a clean integration model that kept S/4HANA as the central record for delivery and freight cost.

Selecting a transportation management solution for an S/4HANA environment is not just about vendor demos or license price. There are various factors to know, starting from how well the system can fit the business processes, how it integrates with S/4HANA, and whether the users can actually adopt and trust it.

Integration: A TMS system that can only exchange the data with S/4HANA in batches is very different from one that works in real time. In transportation, especially for outbound orders and freight settlement, delayed or unclear integration often can lead to manual work, data mismatches, and rework. Changes in demand documents are another aspect of planning, with the source of truth being the ERP system and freight cost record that stay aligned across finance and logistics.

 

Planning: FTL fits well with SAP TM, while LTL depends heavily on rating accuracy and carrier tariff logic. A small parcel is different from both as it requires real-time rate shopping, service level, label generation, and carrier API connectivity at very high speed. The key is to match the right tool to the mode instead of choosing one system to solve for all equally.

 

Carrier Connectivity: Some best-of-breed platforms have strong carrier networks and can connect more easily to booking, tracking, and invoicing processes. A real advantage for companies who have frequent carrier onboarding needs. SAP’s Business Network for Logistics capabilities have improved, but some specialist platforms offer broader carrier reach in some markets.

User Adoption: Transportation planners work under huge time constraints at times, handling exceptions and needing quick turnaround; they need a system that is intuitive and reliable. If the user interface is too complex, planners will build workarounds in spreadsheets and phone calls, which will defeat the purpose of the whole solution. End-user testing being part of the evaluation is not always a decision for later.

The best transportation solution is one that matches the freight complexity, integrates cleanly with S/4HANA, manages master data well, keeps total cost under control, and is actually usable by the planners who run the business every day.

After the evaluation, the client chose a layered solution instead of trying to use one system for everything. This decision helped the freight network that was too mixed and complex in a single platform approach.

For FTL and international multi-modal freight, they selected SAP TM embedded in S/4HANA. This made sense because their network included ocean and air freight across 14 countries, multi-leg chains, and customs milestones. SAP TM fit that complexity well, and since the master data governance in S/4HANA was already strong, they did not want to add the additional burden of managing a separate transportation system.

For LTL, they kept SAP TM as the main execution platform but connected it to a third-party LTL rating engine through an API. This gave them more accurate carrier-specific pricing and better handling of accessorials while still keeping the S/4HANA delivery document as the main record. The integration worked well because the rating engine acted like a service rather than trying to own the planning process itself.

For small parcels, they chose a dedicated parcel management solution and connected to S/HANA using outbound delivery data. This platform handled real-time rate shopping across multiple carriers and label generation and carrier-specific compliance needs as well. Shipment cost and tracking information flowed back into S/HANA so that finance and customer service still have visibility and also transmit to end customers in the system.

One of the vendors claimed it could cover all three modes, but the proof of concept showed the LTL rating logic could not handle it without manual interference and was easy to knock down.

This is not a recommendation for every company. An organization with simple domestic FTL and little or no parcel volume might do just fine with one good TMS. But the right choice always depends on the company’s freight mix, how complex the network is, how much IT support is available, and how solid the master data is.

The main idea is simple: Use SAP TM where it fits best, use specialized tools where they bring real value, and keep S/4HANA as the core system that ties everything together.

entry.title: ABAP AI: Creating Custom Generative AI Scenarios in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence in ABAP – Ready for Today

Artificial Intelligence capabilities are no longer limited to external services or side-by-side extensions. With recent SAP S/4HANA releases, AI scenarios can be consumed and modeled directly within ABAP, allowing existing applications and processes to be enhanced with Generative AI in an enterprise-ready way.

At the center of this enablement is the ABAP AI SDK powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM). It provides the runtime APIs and lifecycle concepts required to define, manage, and consume AI scenarios from ABAP code in a consistent manner. This makes it possible for customers and partners to build Custom AI scenarios inside their own SAP S/4HANA systems, closely aligned with business logic and system architecture.

Why ABAP AI SDK and ISLM Matter

The ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM, enables ABAP developers to interact with Generative AI models without embedding provider-specific logic into applications. ISLM acts as the central framework and lifecycle component for AI scenarios, while the ABAP AI SDK exposes standardized APIs to consume these scenarios at runtime.

Beyond pure large language model (LLM) interactions, SAP AI Core provides an orchestration service. Orchestration in SAP AI Core is a managed service that enables unified access, control, and execution of generative AI models through standardized APIs, prompt templating, and configurable AI workflow components.

Using orchestration, LLM interactions can be enriched with additional capabilities such as data masking, content filtering, document grounding, and translation. The ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM in ABAP can leverage both direct LLM interactions and orchestration features, allowing ABAP applications to consume advanced AI workflows while keeping governance and lifecycle management aligned with SAP standards. 

These capabilities are available as of SAP S/4 HANA 2023.

Learn by Doing: An SAP Developer Mission

To provide a structured, hands-on entry point, we offer an SAP Developer Mission that guides you through the setup and usage of Custom AI scenarios in ABAP.

The mission consists of a tutorial group, guiding you how to set up your SAP S/4 HANA system for custom AI scenarios and dedicated tutorials demonstrating how Custom GenAI scenarios are built and consumed from ABAP using the ABAP AI SDK, including orchestration-based interactions with generative models.

Who Is This Relevant For?

The mission is intended for:

  • ABAP developers who want to understand how Generative AI can be consumed from ABAP
  • Architects evaluating AI integration options for SAP S/4HANA
  • Development teams exploring Custom AI scenarios within their existing SAP landscapes

No prior AI expertise is required, but familiarity with ABAP development is assumed.

Getting Started

Ready? Let's explore the SAP Developer Mission 

👉Get Started with GenAI Development in SAP S/4HANA 

which provides a guided and practical introduction on  Generative AI scenario creation and consumption in ABAP.

Want to Learn More?

  • SAP Help Portal: ABAP AI & ISLM Documentation
  • SAP Community Page: Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management for SAP S/4HANA 
  • SAP Community Blog: Scaling Generative AI in SAP S/4HANA Enterprise-Ready Innovations with ISLM 
  • SAP Community Blog: Developing AI‑enabled applications with the ABAP AI SDK (Public Cloud & Steampunk)

entry.title: Introducing SAP Note 3690029 – Integration Technologies and Frameworks in the Context of Clean Core
entry.content:
SAP Note 3690029 – Integration Technologies and Frameworks in Context of Clean Core Integration is now available. This blog post gives a quick overview of the note, the reasoning behind it, and how it fits next to its well-known sibling, SAP Note 3578329 for extensibility.

Why a dedicated integration note?

When customers and partners plan their journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition (and ultimately to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition), one question keeps coming back:

"I want to use interface technology X – is that still considered Clean Core?"

Until now, the only widely referenced answer was SAP Note 3578329 – Frameworks, Technologies and Development Patterns in Context of Clean Core Extensibility. That note is excellent – but it was written for extensibility use cases. In day-to-day projects it has been increasingly applied to integration scenarios as well, which led to misinterpretations, and a fair amount of uncertainty in the field about what is and isn't a clean integration.

A dedicated, official statement for integration technologies was needed and that is what SAP Note 3690029 delivers.

What does the new note provide?

SAP Note 3690029 introduces a level-based Clean Core integration model and classifies the most commonly used interface technologies, protocols, and patterns according to two criteria:

  1. Public Cloud readiness: is the technology supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition?
  2. Modern integration standards: is it based on established cloud and internet standards, and does it align with modern approaches like event-driven architecture (EDA) and an API-managed strategy?

Each technology is rated on the same A / B / C / D scale already known from the extensibility note:

  • Level A – Strategic, cloud-ready, modern. Always the first choice
  • Level B – Works today, but plan to move on. Limited cloud readiness or limited alignment with modern standards
  • Level C – Legacy, no cloud migration path. Only acceptable in clearly bounded on-premise scenarios
  • Level D – Avoid in any new design

The same simple rules apply as for extensibility:

  • Stay clean: choose the highest level available for the use case.
  • Get clean: from the current level, plan the move to the next higher one.

How is this different from SAP Note 3578329?

The two notes complement each other and do not overlap. If a framework or technology touches both worlds (a classic example: IDoc integration combined with an IDoc extension), the rule of thumb is simple:

  • SAP Note 3690029 is used to choose the right integration technology.
  • SAP Note 3578329 is used to implement the extension in a clean way.
Clean Core principleIntegrationExtensibility
Typical question"How is S/4HANA accessed from another system?""How is S/4HANA itself extended?"
Example use caseCalling a released OData / web service / IDoc / BAPI from an external systemImplementing a BAdI, creating a custom CDS view

The decision tree below(also attached to the note captures the flow at a glance:

Decision tree – when to apply SAP Note 3690029 vs. SAP Note 3578329

  1. Is a released API/event on SAP Business Accelerator Hub or a standard SAP integration scenario available and sufficient? → Use it, Cleanest possible option.
  2. If not, hand over to extensibility for any local change. If standard extension points exist, they should be used and the Cloudification Repository should be followed.
  3. If no SAP standard content exists at all, SAP Note 3578329 should be followed, and only as a last resort should a custom interface be built, still respecting Clean Core extensibility guidelines.

How to use the note in projects

A few practical recommendations:

  • Apply it during integration assessment, Whenever a new interface is scoped, the technology should be looked up in the table and the resulting Clean Core level documented as part of the architecture decision.
  • Reassess regularly, Released APIs and events are continuously added to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. What is Level B today might have a Level A successor tomorrow.
  • Combine the note with the right tooling:
    • SAP Business Accelerator Hub – the catalog of released remote APIs and events, including documentation and versioning.
    • SAP Help Portal – integration and modernization guides plus release information for SAP S/4HANA remote APIs and business events.
    • Cloudification Repository – object-level release state and Clean Core classification
  • Do not forget upgrade stability. Because upgrade stability depends on the release status of the underlying ABAP object, it is intentionally not part of the integration level rating. Cross-checking with SAP Business Accelerator Hub, the Cloudification Repository, and SAP Note 3578329 is recommended.

What's next?

The note is a living document. The current version focuses on integration technologies available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition. Future updates will:

  • Expand the table to additional interface technologies, including data integration
